title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Install all required packages for flow matching, DiT model, and evaluation metrics.

steps.step2: Install PyTorch with CUDA: pip install torch torchvision
steps.step3: Install dependencies: pip install diffusers transformers accelerate einops timm
steps.step4: Install evaluation: pip install pytorch-fid lpips
steps.step5: Install utilities: pip install numpy scipy matplotlib seaborn tqdm ptflops
steps.step6: Verify GPU: python -c 'import torch; print(f"CUDA: {torch.cuda.is_available()}, GPU: {torch.cuda.get_device_name(0)}")'
steps.step7: Create directories: mkdir -p src data checkpoints logs figures results samples

title: Download CIFAR-10 and Prepare Data Loaders
description: Download CIFAR-10 dataset and create flow matching data loaders with (x0, x1, t) triplets.

steps.step1: Download CIFAR-10: python -c "import torchvision; torchvision.datasets.CIFAR10(root='./data/cifar10', train=True, download=True); torchvision.datasets.CIFAR10(root='./data/cifar10', train=False, download=True)"
steps.step3: Implement flow matching: x_t = (1-t)*x0 + t*x1, target v = x1 - x0
steps.step4: Test loader: verify batch shapes (B,3,32,32) for CIFAR-10

title: Implement DiT-S/2 Base Architecture
description: Implement DiT-S/2 (Small, patch size 2) for CIFAR-10: 12 layers, 384 dim, 6 heads, ~33M params.

steps.step1: Create src/dit.py with DiT model adapted for flow matching
steps.step3: Conditioning: Timestep + class embedding via AdaLN
steps.step4: Output: Predicted velocity v_theta(x_t, t, c)
steps.step5: Test forward pass and count parameters (~33M expected)

title: Train Baseline DiT (No Routing)
description: Train standard DiT-S/2 on CIFAR-10 as quality baseline. 50 epochs gives good convergence within time budget.

steps.step2: Config: batch_size=128, lr=1e-4, AdamW, 50 epochs (~20K steps)
steps.step3: Train on CIFAR-10 (~60 min on A6000)
steps.step4: Validate every 10 epochs, save best checkpoint
steps.step6: Final eval: generate 5000 images, compute FID
steps.step7: Measure FLOPs per sample using ptflops

title: Random Token Dropping and Uniform Skipping
description: Evaluate two simple baselines: random token dropping and uniform layer skipping. These test if learned routing is meaningful.

steps.step1: Implement random token dropping: keep ratios [0.5, 0.33, 0.25]
steps.step2: Implement uniform layer skipping: keep [50%, 33%] of layers
steps.step3: Load pretrained baseline, apply dropping/skipping during inference
steps.step5: Compute FID, FLOPs, time for each
steps.step7: Record best performing baseline for comparison

title: Implement FlowRouter Architecture
description: Implement FlowRouter with flow-guided token routing: routers use [features, timestep, velocity magnitude] to decide which tokens process through each layer.

steps.step2: Router MLP: 2 layers, hidden=128, inputs=[h_i, t_emb, ||v_i||]
steps.step3: Routing: tokens with score >= tau(t) process through DiT block, others skip
steps.step5: FLOPs counter: track actual compute based on routing decisions
steps.step6: Parameter check: routers <2% of base model (~0.5M)
steps.step7: Test forward pass and gradient flow

title: Implement Training Objectives
description: Implement three loss components: reconstruction (flow matching), velocity consistency, and computation budget.

steps.step1: L_rec = MSE(v_pred, x1-x0) - standard flow matching
steps.step2: L_vel = ||v_{l+1} - v_l||^2 for skipped tokens - trajectory smoothness
steps.step3: L_budget = max(0, FLOPs_actual - FLOPs_target)^2 - budget constraint
steps.step6: - Router warmup: 5 epochs, freeze base, lr=1e-3
steps.step7: - Joint training: 20 epochs, base lr=1e-5, router lr=1e-4

title: Train FlowRouter (Primary Result)
description: Train FlowRouter to achieve 2x FLOPs reduction on CIFAR-10. This is our main result.

steps.step2: Router warmup (5 epochs, ~6 min): freeze base, train routers to 80% FLOPs
steps.step3: Joint training (20 epochs, ~24 min): target 50% FLOPs (2x speedup)
steps.step4: Validate every 5 epochs: FID at current FLOPs
steps.step5: Save best checkpoint at target budget
steps.step7: Log routing stats: skip rates, velocity correlations

title: Evaluate FlowRouter Performance
description: Comprehensive evaluation of FlowRouter against baselines with all metrics.

steps.step7: - Skip rates per layer and timestep
steps.step8: - Correlation: velocity magnitude vs routing depth

title: Ablation Studies (3 Ablations in Parallel)
description: Test contribution of each novel component: velocity signal, consistency loss, adaptive threshold. All 3 ablations run with same (reduced) training time.

steps.step1: Ablation 1 - No Velocity Signal: router input=[features, timestep] only
steps.step3: Ablation 3 - Fixed Threshold: constant tau instead of tau(t)
steps.step4: Each ablation: 15 epochs training (~20 min each)

title: Routing Pattern Visualization
description: Visualize where computation is allocated: spatial heatmaps, timestep patterns, velocity correlation.

steps.step2: Create Figure: spatial heatmap of average routing depth
steps.step3: Create Figure: routing rate vs timestep
steps.step4: Create Figure: scatter plot velocity magnitude vs routing depth
steps.step5: Analyze: do complex regions (edges, textures) get more compute?

title: Statistical Analysis and Success Criteria
description: Verify statistical significance and check all success criteria from proposal.

steps.step1: Collect results across 3 seeds for all methods
steps.step2: Compute mean ± std for FID, FLOPs, time
steps.step4: Check Criterion 1: >2x FLOPs reduction with <5 FID increase
steps.step5: Check Criterion 2: Simple trajectories (low velocity var) -> fewer layers
steps.step7: Check Criterion 4: Routing varies across timesteps

title: Generate Paper Figures
description: Create publication-quality figures and tables for the paper.

steps.step1: Figure 1: FID vs FLOPs curve (main result with error bars)
steps.step2: Figure 2: Qualitative comparison (baseline vs FlowRouter samples)
steps.step4: Figure 4: Ablation study (bar chart of component contributions)

title: Compile Final Results
description: Compile all results into structured summary for paper writing.

steps.step1: Create results/results_summary.json with all key metrics
steps.step2: Include: main results, baselines, ablations, statistical tests
